zh.lifeSummary: Sir Francis Empson是Sir Henry Empson和Thomasine Melton的兒子，於1390年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾堂可斯特。他於1414年與Margaret Burdon在英國諾薩普頓史爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1495年11月9日在英國諾廷加姆爾霍爾梅 皮朋特享年105歲。

nameMeanings.name: Empson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: metronymic meaning ‘son of Emma’, from the Middle English female personal name Em(e), Emme (see Imm ) + -son.
shortened form of Emerson . The two names were no doubt confused, and it is possible that the reverse process sometimes occurred, whereby Em(m)erson was an altered form of Emmeson, i.e. Em(p)son.

variants.name: Imeson
meanings: English: variant either of Emmeson (see Empson ) or of Emerson .

variants.name: Impson
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Empson  . This form of the surname is now more common in the US than in Britain.

variants.name: Imm
meanings: German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Im(m)o, a pet form of names based on Irmin-, a divine or ethnic name formed with irmin, ermen ‘whole, entire’.

English (Wiltshire): from the ancient Germanic female personal name Imma, Emma, of the same origin as in 1 above. In Old English Imma, Emma was borne by both males and females. In Middle English, under Norman influence, the personal name Emm(a), Imm(a) came to be used almost exclusively for women, being taken as a short form of Ermingard.

nameMeanings.name: Francis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of Italian Francesco , originally a vocabulary word meaning ‘French’ or ‘Frenchman’ (Late Latin Franciscus; compare Frank ). This was a nickname given to St Francis of Assisi ( 1181–1226 ) because of his wealthy father's business connections with France. His baptismal name was Giovanni . He had a pleasant, ordinary life as a child and young man, but after two serious illnesses, a period of military service, and a year as a prisoner of war in Perugia, he turned from the world and devoted himself to caring for the poor and sick. He was joined by groups of disciples, calling themselves ‘minor friars’ (friari minores). The main features of the Franciscan rule are humility, poverty, and love for all living creatures. The given name occurs occasionally in England as early as 1300 , and more frequently from the early 16th century, when there was a surge of admiration for, and imitation of, Italian Renaissance culture.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Francis Empson was born in 1390, in Towcester, Northamptonshire, England as the son of Sir Henry Empson and Thomasine Melton. He married Margaret Burdon in 1414, in Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 9 November 1495, in Holme Pierrepont, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 105.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Francis Empsonは1390年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアトウセスターでSir Henry EmpsonとThomasine Meltonの息子として生まれた。1414年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアでMargaret Burdonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1495年11月9日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアホールメ ピエレプオントで105歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Francis Empson(은)는 1390년 영국 노르섬프토실르 타우스스터에서 Sir Henry Empson(와)과 Thomasine Melton의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1414년 영국 노르섬프토실르에서 Margaret Burdon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1495년 11월 9일 영국 노틴그함실르 홀 피레폰트에서 105세의 나이로 사망했다.
